core_payload.content:
# Tile Layout Algorithm Concept

## 1. Objective
Design an algorithmic strategy for tile patterns that balances aesthetics, installation complexity, and material efficiency.

## 2. Strategies
Use deterministic pattern generators, constraint aware boundary handling, and waste estimation with configurable margins.

## 3. Evaluation
Every layout should emit metrics such as cut count, edge complexity, estimated waste, and labor intensity.

## 4. Research
Explore hybrid methods that combine deterministic templates with local optimization on irregular geometry.

neuro_concentrate.summary: This active concept capsule defines the algorithmic design space for tile layout generation in TileSims. It combines deterministic pattern templates with geometry constraints and cost metrics to compare alternatives objectively. The concept requires each candidate layout to produce explainable measures including cut count, waste estimate, and expected labor complexity so users can evaluate tradeoffs. It also proposes hybrid approaches that preserve predictable template behavior while applying local optimization to difficult boundaries, where naive placement strategies typically degrade both material efficiency and visual quality.
